metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4955
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The authors developed an optical imaging assay in primary cell culture and first loaded the recycling synaptic vesicles with FM4-64 dye during 60 s of 20 Hz electrical stimulation (Gaffield and Betz, 2006; see Figure 7-figure supplement 1 for example images) and then monitored destaining with time-lapse fluorescence imaging during low frequency (0.2 Hz) stimulation in the absence of dye, followed by near complete destaining with a second 20 Hz train. The results indicate that destaining at 0.2Hz is significantly increased in synapses of different null mutants (SYP1,2 SNG1,3), most importantly between QKO and TKO + SNG3. Given other evidence in the same study (Fig 2-6), the data indicate that SNG3 is a negative regulator of exocytosis, and especially of fusogenicity, suggesting a role in the priming steps.

Protein names; SYP1 = synaptophysin/SYP; SYP2 = synaptophysin-2 aka. synaptoporin/SYNPR; SYG1 = synaptogyrin-1/SYNGR1; SYG3 = synaptogyrin-3/SYNGR3.. Experimental description: synaptogyrin 3 antibody confirmed knockout of SNG3 (Fig 1, mouse line Jackson labs 008415)
Synaptogyrin 3 antibody RRID:AB_2619752 Synaptic Sys. rabbit 1:1000
